title: Verification purity and explicit publication
goal: Make daemon-owned verification observationally pure and make completion publication stage only explicit daemon-owned paths, with behavior-first regressions for verification side effects and publication confinement.
acceptance: Use the confirmed behavioral TDD seams: first add focused gate/module tests plus daemon black-box publication tests, demonstrate that they fail for the intended side-effect/publication reason, then implement without weakening the assertions.

Before and after every slice and integration verification command, the daemon compares a NUL-safe Git worktree snapshot that distinguishes tracked modifications, staged changes, untracked files, renames, and deletions; paths containing whitespace or newlines are handled correctly.

A verification command that changes the verified worktree is classified as failed verification, records structured before/after evidence, restores the worktree to its exact pre-command state, and never lets those mutations reach merge, repair, completion publication, or final_sha.
Successful verification requires the expected clean integration workspace; ignored build outputs may remain ignored but cannot become publication inputs.

Completion publication replaces unrestricted git add -A with an explicit daemon-owned publication manifest. Only close-record slice JSON and declared report/handoff publication paths are stageable; unrelated tracked or untracked files cannot be committed.

Verification-cache reuse has an explicit tested identity including repository/worktree identity, verified commit/tree, command, cwd, environment, and relevant policy version; commands with side effects or changed identity are not falsely reused.
Regressions cover a mutating successful command, a mutating failing command, an unrelated dirty publication file, NUL-sensitive path names, restoration failure, and a normal successful publication.
Pi remains the sole real worker harness, verification remains daemon-owned, and slice verification remains within slice authority.
must_ask_if: The fix would make workers, CLI renderers, Herdr, or Pi own verification or publication decisions.
Exact restoration cannot be guaranteed without deleting pre-existing operator changes or ignored dependency/build caches.
The implementation would disable verification caching globally instead of making its safety policy explicit and testable.
An existing test must be deleted, ignored, or relaxed to pass.
